content.full_text:
**State of California  
Fair Political Practices Commission**

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

September 23, 1976

EXEMPTION LETTER

Mr. George H. Murphy  
Secretary, California Commission  
on Uniform State Laws  
Legislative Counsel  
3021 State Capitol  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Murphy:

Thank you for your letter of July 26, 1976, on behalf of the members of the California Commission on Uniform State Laws with attached Request for Exemption from Government Code Section 87300, requiring each state agency to adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code.

Based on the facts presented in your request for exemption, a review of the statutory authority under which your agency operates, and personal discussions with you by a member of our staff, the Fair Political Practices Commission finds, pursuant to the provisions of 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18751(b)(1), that if the Commission on Uniform State Laws were to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code, there would be no "designated employees", within the meaning of Government Code §§82019 and 87302(a), subject to its provisions. Accordingly, your Request for Exemption from Government Code §87300 is approved.

Should the statutory authority or the factual circumstances under which your agency operates and on which this exemption is based change, you have the responsibility to inform this agency immediately. The exempt status of your agency then will be subject to review, either at the request of your agency or on the initiative of this Commission.

If we may be of further assistance to you, please let us know.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief  
Conflicts of Interest Division

EAK:mra

California Commission on Uniform State Laws

July 26, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Commissioners:

On behalf of the members of the Commission on Uniform State Laws, I request an exemption from the provisions of Section 87300 of the Government Code, requiring each state agency to adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code.

Pursuant to your regulation (Sec. 18751, 2 Cal. Adm. C.) the following items are enclosed: (1) a list of every position in the agency and the job description of each position; (2) a copy of the statutory authority under which the commission was created, with the duties and responsibilities of the commission in emphasis; (3) identification of the body to whom the commission reports; (4) a copy of the commission's last report; and (5) a justification of the request for exemption.

Sincerely,

George H. Murphy
Secretary, California Commission on Uniform State Laws
